reasoning: As of November 23, 2025, the "Coalition of the Willing" (officially planning to deploy the "Multinational Force Ukraine") has explicitly stated that its forces—intended as a "reassurance" rather than a war-fighting entity—will only deploy "once hostilities have ceased" and a ceasefire is established (search 3). Currently, peace plans are merely at the proposal and counter-proposal stage (e.g., Trump's 28-point plan released on Nov 21 and the European counter-proposal on Nov 23). Given that Donald Trump doesn't take office until January 2026, and the complexities of reaching an agreement and scaling down hostilities involving Russia and Ukraine, achieving a finalized peace deal, an active ceasefire, and the actual physical deployment of a multinational force into Ukraine all within the 5 remaining weeks of 2025 is logistically and politically implausible. Therefore, the probability of deployment on or before December 31, 2025, is extremely low.
